TEXT.English: Mad dogs coming directly from the hell's mouth. At least, according to skeptics, that is what their owners would have you believe.
TEXT.French: Des Chiens enragĂ©s venant directement de la gueule de l'enfer. Du moins, selon les sceptiques, c'est ce que leurs propriĂ©taires voudraient vous faire croire.
TEXT.Italian: Un cane impazzito che proviene direttamente dalla bocca dell'inferno; o secondo gli scettici, questo Ăš ciĂČ che i loro possessori vogliono far credere.
TEXT.Polish: Wœciek³e psy hodowane przez œwi¹tynie Voodoo.
TEXT.Russian: ĂĂ°ĂźĂ±ĂČĂ­Ă»Ă„ ĂĄĂ„Ă±ĂČĂšĂš, ĂŻĂ°ĂšĂ”ĂźĂ€ĂżĂčĂšĂ„ Ă­Ă„ĂŻĂźĂ±Ă°Ă„Ă€Ă±ĂČĂąĂ„Ă­Ă­Ăź ĂšĂ§ ĂŁĂ«ĂłĂĄĂšĂ­ Ă Ă€Ă . ĂĂź ĂȘĂ°Ă Ă©Ă­Ă„Ă© ĂŹĂ„Ă°Ă„, ĂŻĂź Ă±Ă«ĂźĂąĂ ĂŹ Ă±ĂȘĂ„ĂŻĂČĂšĂȘĂźĂą, ĂšĂ” Ă”ĂźĂ§ĂżĂ„ĂąĂ Ă”ĂźĂČĂ„Ă«Ăš ĂĄĂ» Ă§Ă Ă±ĂČĂ ĂąĂšĂČĂŒ ĂąĂ Ă± ĂŻĂźĂąĂ„Ă°ĂšĂČĂŒ Ăą Ă°Ă„Ă Ă«ĂŒĂ­ĂźĂ±ĂČĂŒ ĂŻĂźĂ€ĂźĂĄĂ­Ă»Ă” ĂŹĂźĂ­Ă±ĂČĂ°ĂźĂą.

TEXT.English: While Missionaries often focus on extolling the glories of their respective gods to spread their religion, the Inquisitor focuses instead on pointing out the fallacies of all religions but his own. Ardent in the belief that all other religions are wrong, the Inquisitor lives to destroy the beliefs of others. If the inquisition convicted a person of heresy, the persoanl property of a condemned person was confiscated and places of worship were destroyed; based on medieval and feudal laws. Usually the confiscated property was divided three ways: by the Inquisition members, the church officials, and the state treasury. The Church and state kept strict accounts of the prisoner's incarceration, trial and execution costs. If the value of his confiscated property was not enough to cover these costs, then his heirs had to pay the difference. Records show many women and children were left penniless from such confiscation. You may expend an Inquisitor inside a city to remove all religions from that city except your own.
TEXT.French: Les missionnaires se contentent souvent de glorifier leurs dieux respectifs pour propager leur religion, alors que l'inquisiteur s'efforce de dĂ©montrer les aberrations de toutes les autres religions. Convaincu que toutes les autres religions sont dans l'erreur, l'inquisiteur vit pour dĂ©truire les croyances des autres. Vous pouvez envoyer un inquisiteur dans une ville pour l'expurger de toutes les religions sauf la vĂŽtre.
TEXT.German: WĂ€hrend Missionare sich oft darauf konzentrieren, die Tugenden ihrer jeweiligen GĂ¶tter zu ĂŒbertreiben, um ihre Religion zu verbreiten, konzentriert sich der Inquisitor stattdessen darauf, die Fehler aller anderen Religionen auĂer seiner eigenen aufzuzeigen. Der Inquisitor brennt vor Ăberzeugung, dass alle anderen Religionen falsch sind, und lebt dafĂŒr, den Glauben aller anderen zu zerstĂ¶ren. Sie kĂ¶nnen einen Inquisitor in eine Stadt entsenden, um aus dieser Stadt alle Religionen auĂer Ihrer zu entfernen.
TEXT.Italian: Mentre i missionari si concentrano sulle lodi delle rispettive divinitĂ per diffondere la propria religione, gli inquisitori mettono in risalto gli errori delle altre religioni. Credono ardentemente che le altre religioni si sbaglino e vivono per distruggere la fede degli altri. Puoi usare un Inquisitore in una cittĂ per rimuoverne tutte le religioni tranne la tua.
TEXT.Spanish: Mientras que los misioneros suelen concentrarse en cantar las glorias de sus respectivos dioses para difundir su fe, los inquisidores se dedican a poner de manifiesto las falacias de todas las demĂĄs religiones. Ferviente convencido de que todas las demĂĄs religiones se equivocan, El inquisidor vive para socavar las creencias de los demĂĄs. Puedes gastar un inquisidor dentro de una ciudad para eliminar todas las religiones salvo la tuya.
TEXT.Polish: Inkwizycja (³ac. Inquisitio? œledztwo, badanie)? potoczna nazwa systemu œledczo-s¹downiczego Koœcio³a katolickiego dzia³aj¹cego od XIII do XIX wieku, utworzonego w celu wyszukiwania, nawracania i karania heretyków w oparciu o postanowienia ujête w dokumentach soborowych, synodalnych oraz bullach papieskich.

TEXT.English: [BOLD]Strategy:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]While not particularly effective against defenders in cities, these mid-level horse units get a +25% bonus when fighting melee or archery units. Thus they can be especially effective against non-horse units in open terrain.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Background:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Before the invention of the stirrup, riders stayed atop their horses by knee pressure alone. Close combat was virtually impossible, as the shock from a blow - taken OR given - would very likely pitch the rider onto the ground. While the earliest stirrups appeared around 500 BC, they didn't reach Europe until the sixth or seventh century AD. At this point the European Light Cavalry was born. While lightly armored when compared with Heavy Cavalry or Knights, a Light Cavalry unit can still deliver a shocking blow to infantry or siege units.
TEXT.French: [BOLD]StratĂ©gie:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Bien qu'elles ne soient pas particuliĂšrement efficaces contre les dĂ©fenseurs des villes, ces unitĂ©s montĂ©es de niveau moyen obtiennent un bonus de 25 % contre les unitĂ©s de combat rapprochĂ© et les archers. Elles peuvent donc ĂȘtre trĂšs efficaces contre les unitĂ©s Ă pied en terrain dĂ©gagĂ©.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Histoire:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Avant l'invention des Ă©triers, les cavaliers se maintenaient sur leur monture simplement Ă l'aide de leurs genoux. Le combat rapprochĂ© Ă©tait quasiment impossible, car le choc d'un coup donnĂ© ou reĂ§u avait toutes les chances de dĂ©sarĂ§onner le cavalier. Les premiers Ă©triers apparurent vers le IVe siĂšcle av JC, mais n'arrivĂšrent pas en Europe avant le VIe ou le VIIe siĂšcle. C'est alors que la cavalerie lĂ©gĂšre europĂ©enne fit son apparition. MĂȘme s'ils ont une armure bien plus lĂ©gĂšre que les cavaliers lourds ou les chevaliers, les cavaliers lĂ©gers peuvent ĂȘtre trĂšs dangereux pour les unitĂ©s de siĂšge ou d'infanterie.
TEXT.German: [BOLD]Strategie:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Auch wenn sie nicht besonders effektiv im Einsatz gegen Verteidigungseinheiten in StĂ€dten sind, erhalten diese mittelklassigen berittenen Einheiten einen 25%igen Bonus, wenn sie im Nahkampf oder gegen BogenschĂŒtzen eingesetzt werden. Sie kĂ¶nnen also besonders effektiv gegen unberittene Einheiten auf offenem Feld sein.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Hintergrund:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Vor der Erfindung der SteigbĂŒgel hielten sich Reiter allein durch Druck ihrer Knie im Sattel. Ein Nahkampf war so nahezu unmĂ¶glich, da die ErschĂŒtterung eines Schlages - ausgeteilt wie eingesteckt - den Reiter wahrscheinlich auf den Boden geschleudert hĂ€tte. Auch wenn die ersten SteigbĂŒgel bereits aus dem Jahre 500 v.Chr. datieren, waren sie in Europa nicht vor dem 6. oder 7. Jahrhundert n.Chr. zu finden. Zu dieser Zeit wurde die europĂ€ische Leichte Kavallerie geboren. Auch wenn sie im Vergleich zur schweren Kavallerie oder den Rittern nur sehr leicht gepanzert waren, kann die Leichte Kavallerie der Infanterie oder Belagerungseinheiten einen schweren Schlag verpassen.
TEXT.Italian: [BOLD]Strategia:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Pur non essendo particolarmente efficaci contro i difensori nelle cittĂ , queste unitĂ a cavallo di medio livello ottengono un +25% di bonus quando combattono unitĂ da combattimento ravvicinato o arcieri. Sono quindi particolarmente efficaci contro unitĂ non montate su terreno aperto.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Informazioni:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Prima dell'invenzione della staffa, i cavalieri rimanevano a cavallo unicamente grazie alla pressione delle ginocchia. Il combattimento ravvicinato era impossibile, visto che con un colpo (dato o ricevuto) il cavaliere sarebbe probabilmente caduto a terra. Pur essendo comparse attorno al 500 a.C., le staffe non raggiunsero l'Europa se non durante il sesto o settimo secolo d.C. A questo punto nacque la cavalleria leggera. Pur essendo poco corazzata, se confrontata con la cavalleria pesante o coi cavalieri, un'unitĂ di cavalleria leggera puĂČ comunque infliggere colpi pesanti alla fanteria o a unitĂ d'assedio.
TEXT.Spanish: [BOLD]Estrategia:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Aunque no son especialmente eficaces para atacar ciudades, estas unidades de caballerĂ­a reciben una bonificaciĂłn del 25%% frente a unidades de cuerpo a cuerpo o de arqueros. Por ello, pueden resultar letales contra unidades no montadas a campo abierto.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Historia:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Antes de la invenciĂłn del estribo, los jinetes se mantenĂ­an sobre los caballos utilizando solo la presiĂłn de las rodillas. Entrar en combate cuerpo a cuerpo era prĂĄcticamente imposible para este tipo de unidades, puesto que era muy probable que el impacto de un golpe, ya fuera recibido o infligido, arrojase al jinete al suelo. Aunque los primeros estribos aparecieron alrededor del aĂ±o 500 a. C., en Europa no hicieron su apariciĂłn hasta los siglos VI o VII d. C. Ăse fue el nacimiento de la caballerĂ­a ligera europea. Aunque estĂĄ menos armada y peor equipada que la caballerĂ­a pesada o los caballeros, la caballerĂ­a ligera puede ser letal frente a las unidades de infanterĂ­a o de asedio.
TEXT.Polish: W Europie we wczesnym œredniowieczu w okresie wczesnofeudalnym jazda zorganizowana by³a jako przyboczny oddzia³ w³adcy (ksiêcia, kniazia). By³a czêœci¹ dru¿yny ksi¹¿êcej, która istnia³a zarówno wœród prymitywnych plemion germañskich, jak i s³owiañskich (w tych ostatnich pojawi³a siê nieco póŸniej). W Polsce, w okresie rz¹dów pierwszych Piastów w sk³ad dru¿yny ksi¹¿êcej wchodzi³a jazda ciê¿ka (tzw. Pancerni) oraz jazda lekka.

TEXT.English: [BOLD]Strategy:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]These early sword units can attack enemy cities that are not too heavily defended. Of course it's very useful to bring siege equipment when trying to take cities.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Background:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]During the eighth century AD, European swords tended to be long and straight, with no crossguard to protect the wielder's hand (crossguards didn't appear in Europe until the Late Middle Ages). Light Swordsmen are unarmored, or wear leather armor at best.
TEXT.French: [BOLD]StratĂ©gie:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Ces premiĂšres unitĂ©s de spadassins peuvent attaquer les villes ennemies qui ne sont pas trop dĂ©fendues. L'Ă©quipement de siĂšge est bien sĂ»r trĂšs utile pour prendre une ville.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Histoire:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Au cours du VIIIe siĂšcle, les Ă©pĂ©es europĂ©ennes Ă©taient gĂ©nĂ©ralement longues, droites et dĂ©pourvues de garde pour protĂ©ger la main du combattant. La garde n'apparut pas en Europe avant le haut Moyen Age. Les spadassins lĂ©gers ne portent pas d'armure ou au mieux une armure de cuir.
TEXT.German: [BOLD]Strategie:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Diese frĂŒhen Schwerteinheiten kĂ¶nnen feindliche StĂ€dte angreifen, die nicht allzu gut gesichert sind. Es ist natĂŒrlich immer nĂŒtzlich, BelagerungsausrĂŒstung mit sich zu fĂŒhren, wenn man StĂ€dte einnimmt.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Hintergrund:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]WĂ€hrend des 8. Jahrhunderts waren die Schwerter in Europa lang und gerade und hatten keine Parierstange, um die Hand des KĂ€mpfers zu schĂŒtzen. (Parierstangen waren in Europa nicht vor dem spĂ€ten Mittelalter zu finden.) Leichte SchwertkĂ€mpfer sind ungepanzert oder tragen hĂ¶chsten eine leichte LederrĂŒstung.
TEXT.Italian: [BOLD]Strategia:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Queste prime unitĂ armate con spada possono attaccare cittĂ che non sono pesantemente difese. Chiaramente Ăš sempre utile portare con sĂ© unitĂ d'assedio, quando si cerca di conquistare una cittĂ .[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Informazioni:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Durante l'ottavo secolo d.C., le spade europee tendevano a essere lunghe e diritte, senza guardia per proteggere la mano di chi le impugnava (le guardie non comparvero in Europa fino al tardo Medioevo). Gli spadaccini leggeri sono senza corazza, o al massimo indossano corazze di cuoio.
TEXT.Spanish: [BOLD]Estrategia:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Estas unidades pueden atacar ciudades enemigas, siempre que no estĂ©n demasiado defendidas. En cualquier caso, es muy conveniente utilizar mĂĄquinas de asedio para atacar ciudades.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Historia:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]En el siglo VIII d. C., las espadas europeas solĂ­an ser largas y rectas, y no tenĂ­an guarda para proteger la mano de su propietario (las guardas no aparecieron en Europa hasta la Baja Edad Media). Los soldados con espada ligera combaten sin armadura, o como mucho con armadura de cuero.
TEXT.Polish: W spo³eczeñstwach plemiennych, w toczenie wojen bywa³a zaanga¿owana ca³a mêska czêœæ populacji, np. u Gurkhów, Maorysów oraz w dawnych plemionach celtyckich, germañskich i s³owiañskich. W bardziej rozwiniêtych spo³eczeñstwach wojownicy czêsto tworzyli w³asn¹ kastê lub stan.

TEXT.English: Mesopotamian religion refers to the religious beliefs and practices followed by the Sumerian and East Semitic Akkadian, Assyrian, Babylonian and later migrant Arameans and Chaldeans, living in Mesopotamia (a region encompassing modern Iraq, Kuwait, southeast Turkey and northeast Syria) that dominated the region for a period of 4200 years from the fourth millennium BCE throughout Mesopotamia to approximately the 10th century CE in Assyria. Mesopotamian polytheism was the only religion in ancient Mesopotamia for thousands of years before entering a period of gradual decline beginning between the 1st and 3rd centuries CE

TEXT.English: [BOLD]Strategy:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Mounted Infantry are less powerful than Horse Archers; however, these units do receive terrain defensive bonuses, making them far more powerful when defending in hills, woods, or city.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Background:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Mounted Infantry soldiers use horses to get to the battlefield, but once there they dismount and fight on foot. They rarely take part in charges or other horseback maneuvers, and they tend to suffer badly when engaged in such against trained cavalry units. On the other hand, when fortified on a hill, Mounted Infantry can do serious damage to an attacking Horse Archer or Light Cavalry unit.
TEXT.French: [BOLD]StratĂ©gie:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]L'infanterie montĂ©e est moins puissante que les archers montĂ©s, mais bĂ©nĂ©ficie de bonus dĂ©fensifs de terrain, ce qui lui permet de se dĂ©fendre bien plus efficacement sur des collines, dans les bois ou dans les villes.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Histoire:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Les soldats de l'infanterie montĂ©e se rendent Ă cheval sur le champ de bataille, mais mettent pied Ă terre pour combattre une fois arrivĂ©s Ă destination. Ils participent rarement aux charges ou aux autres manoeuvres Ă cheval, et se font gĂ©nĂ©ralement malmener quand ils le font face Ă des cavaliers entraĂźnĂ©s. En revanche, l'infanterie montĂ©e peut infliger de sĂ©rieux dĂ©gĂąts Ă une unitĂ© de cavalerie lĂ©gĂšre ou d'archers montĂ©s qui l'attaquerait sur une colline oĂč elle aurait pris position.
TEXT.German: [BOLD]Strategie:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Die Berittene Infanterie ist weniger stark als die Berittenen BogenschĂŒtzen, erhĂ€lt jedoch einen GelĂ€nde-Verteidigungsbonus, was sie wesentlich stĂ€rker macht, wenn sie zur Verteidigung in HĂŒgeln, WĂ€ldern oder StĂ€dten eingesetzt wird.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Hintergrund:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Soldaten der Berittenen Infanterie nutzen Pferde, um aufs Schlachtfeld zu gelangen. Sobald sie es erreicht haben, satteln sie jedoch ab und kĂ€mpfen zu FuĂ. Sie nehmen nur selten an VorstĂ¶Ăen oder anderen ManĂ¶vern zu Pferd teil, und wenn sie gegen ausgebildete Einheiten der Kavallerie eingesetzt werden, stecken sie meist viel Schaden ein. Wenn sie gefestigt auf einem HĂŒgel stationiert ist, kann die Berittene Infanterie anrĂŒckenden Berittenen BogenschĂŒtzen oder Einheiten der Leichten Kavallerie jedoch schweren Schaden zufĂŒgen.
TEXT.Italian: [BOLD]Strategia:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]La fanteria a cavallo Ăš meno potente degli arcieri a cavallo, perĂČ queste unitĂ ricevono bonus difensivi per il terreno, che le rendono molto piĂč potenti quando difendono in collina, nelle foreste o in cittĂ .[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Informazioni:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]I soldati della fanteria a cavallo usano i cavalli per arrivare al campo di battaglia ma poi smontano e combattono a piedi. Di rado prendono parte a cariche o altre manovre a cavallo, e tendono a subire malamente gli attacchi delle unitĂ di cavalleria. D'altro canto quando sono fortificate in collina, i fanti a cavallo possono infliggere seri danni ad arcieri a cavallo o cavalleria leggera.
TEXT.Spanish: [BOLD]Estrategia:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]La infanterĂ­a montada es menos potente que los arqueros a caballo; sin embargo, recibe bonificaciones defensivas por terreno, lo que resulta una enorme ventaja a la hora de defenderse en colinas, bosques o ciudades.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Historia:[\BOLD][PARAGRAPH:1]Los soldados de la infanterĂ­a montada usan sus caballos para llegar al campo de batalla, pero una vez allĂ­ desmontan y luchan a pie. Solo en raras ocasiones toman parte en cargas u otras maniobras tĂ­picas de caballerĂ­a y, cuando se enfrentan con escuadrones de caballerĂ­a profesional, normalmente llevan las de perder. Por otro lado, cuando estĂĄ atrincherada en una colina, la infanterĂ­a montada puede ser muy peligrosa para los arqueros a caballo o la caballerĂ­a ligera.
TEXT.Polish: Upadek wartoœci jazdy rycerskiej spowodowa³ powstanie nowych formacji kawaleryjskich w sk³ad których wchodzili ¿o³nierze zaciê¿ni (najemnicy). W XV wieku, obok tradycyjnych kopijników, pojawili siê na polach bitew rajtarzy i arkebuzerzy uzbrojeni w broñ paln¹. W XVI wieku powsta³a dragonia bêd¹ca pocz¹tkowo konn¹ piechot¹. W Europie zachodniej jazda organizowa³a siê na regimenty i kompanie. W Polsce w XV-tym wieku kawaleria dzieli³a siê na oddzia³y kopijnicze i strzelcze. W XVI wieku pojawi³a siê husaria, pocz¹tkowo jazda lekka, z biegiem czasu staj¹c siê najciê¿sz¹ kawaleri¹ polsk¹. Jazdê l¿ejsz¹ w Polsce stanowili pancerni, zwani na Litwie petyhorcami, którzy z czasem stali siê formacj¹ œredniozbrojn¹. Jazdê lekk¹ stanowi³y tzw. chor¹gwie wo³oskie i tatarskie. W XVII wieku na zachodzie pojawili siê kirasjerzy. W XVIII wieku w Polsce dawne pu³ki husarskie i pancerne przekszta³cono kawaleriê narodow¹, a jazdê lekk¹ na nowoczesnych u³anów. W czasach wojen napoleoñskich pojawi³y siê oddzia³y lansjerów, szwole¿erów i strzelców konnych. W XIX wieku w zwi¹zku z dalszym rozwojem broni palnej, znaczenie kawalerii upad³o. Jednak jeszcze w czasie I wojny œwiatowej wojska obu walcz¹cych stron zmobilizowa³y ³¹cznie 63 dywizje jazdy, znaczn¹ ich czêœæ rozwi¹zano zreszt¹ w czasie trwania tej wojny.

TEXT.English: The Bantu mythology is the system of myths and legends of the Bantu peoples of Africa. Although Bantu peoples account for several hundred different ethnic groups, there is a high degree of homogeneity in Bantu cultures and mythologies, just as in Bantu languages. The phrase "Bantu mythology" usually refers to the common, recurring themes that are found in all or most Bantu cultures.
TEXT.French: La mythologie bantoue est l'ensemble des mythes et lĂ©gendes des peuples bantous d'Afrique. Bien que les peuples bantous regroupent plusieurs centaines de groupes ethniques diffĂ©rents, il existe une forte homogĂ©nĂ©itĂ© dans les cultures et mythologies bantoues, tout comme dans les langues bantoues. L'expression "Mythologie bantoue" se rĂ©fĂšre gĂ©nĂ©ralement aux thĂšmes communs et rĂ©currents prĂ©sents dans toutes ou la plupart des cultures bantoues.
TEXT.Polish: Ngai (Enkai, En-kai, Engai, Eng-ai, Mweai, Mwiai) - jedyny bóg w monoteistycznych religiach kenijskich plemion Kamba, Kikuju i Masajów. Wed³ug tradycji Kikuju, mieszka on na œwiêtej górze Kirinyadze (Mount Kenya). Wed³ug Kamba, mieszka gdzieœ w ukryciu i nikt nie wie gdzie. Wed³ug Masajów, Enkai jest nie bogiem, a bogini¹.

TEXT.English: [BOLD]Shaman[\BOLD] perform a plethora of functions depending upon the society wherein they practise their art: healing; leading a sacrifice; preserving the tradition by storytelling and songs; fortune-telling; acting as a psychopomp (literal meaning, âguide of soulsâ). In some cultures, a shaman may fulfill several functions in one person.[NEWLINE][PARAGRAPH:1][BOLD]Shaman[\BOLD] act as "mediators" in their culture. The shaman is seen as communicating with the spirits on behalf of the community, including the spirits of the dead. In some cultures, this mediator function of the shaman may be illustrated well by some of the shaman's objects and symbols.
TEXT.Polish: Szaman - osoba pe³ni¹ca funkcjê religijn¹, polegaj¹c¹ na sformalizowanej i instytucjonalnej wiêzi ekstatycznej z istotami nadludzkimi, duchami przodków lub demonami.

TEXT.English: A kannushi, is the person responsible for the maintenance of a Shinto shrine (jinja) as well as for leading worship of a given kami. The characters for kannushi are sometimes also read jinshu with the same meaning. Originally the kannushi were intermediaries between kami and could transmit their will to common humans. A kannushi was a man capable of miracles or a holy man who, because of his practice of purificatory rites, was capable to work as a medium for a kami, but later the term evolved to being synonymous with shinshoku, that is, a man who works at a shrine and holds religious ceremonies there
TEXT.Polish: Kannushi - kap³an shinto, stra¿nik chramu shintoistycznego. Niegdyœ termin ten odnosi³ siê do g³ównego kap³ana lub kogoœ, kto zosta³ - wed³ug œcis³ych regu³ - wyznaczony do pos³ugi w poœrednictwie z bogami, bóstwami (kami). Zadaniem kannushi jest opiekowanie siê chramem oraz celebrowanie rytua³ów i œwi¹t religijnych, w tym m.in.: obrzêdu odganiania z³ych duchów od nowo narodzonych dzieci przynoszonych przez rodziców do chramu (pod którego s¹ opiek¹) w czasie ich wizyty (miya-mairi), œlubów, rytualnego oczyszczania miejsca, na którym ma byæ zbudowany dom, uczestniczenia w otwieraniu nowych firm itd.

TEXT.English: The Sikh Gurus established Sikhism over the centuries, beginning in the year 1469. Sikhism was founded by the first guru, Guru Nanak, and subsequently, each guru, in succession, was referred to as "Nanak", and as "Light", making their teachings, in the holy scriptures, equivalent. There are a total of 11 gurus: ten human gurus and the eleventh, or current and everlasting Sikh Guru, is the integrated Sikh scriptures known as the Adi Granth or Guru Granth Sahib.
TEXT.French: Les gourous sikhs ont Ă©tabli le sikhisme au fil des siĂšcles, Ă partir de l'an 1469. Le sikhisme a Ă©tĂ© fondĂ© par le premier gourou, Guru Nanak, et par la suite, chaque gourou, successivement, Ă©tait appelĂ© "Nanak" et "LumiĂšre", rendant leurs enseignements dans les Ă©critures sacrĂ©es Ă©quivalents. Il y a en tout 11 gourous : dix gourous humains et le onziĂšme, ou gourou sikh actuel et Ă©ternel, est l'intĂ©gration des Ă©critures sikhes connues sous le nom Adi Granth ou Guru Granth Sahib.

Traktat Gurugita opisuje cechy, rolê oraz w³aœciw¹ relacjê pomiêdzy uczniami a guru. Koniecznoœæ rozwijania zwi¹zku z guru trwaj¹cego nawet po jego œmierci jako przewodnikiem duchowym podkreœla zarówno joga jak i tantra oraz wszystkie pozosta³e nurty hinduistyczne. Rozwijanie w³aœciwego zwi¹zku z ¿yj¹cym mistrzem-przewodnikiem nazywane jest praktyk¹ gurujogi. Gurugita jak i inne traktaty wymieniaj¹ osiem rodzajów guru o coraz wy¿szych umiejêtnoœciach i coraz bardziej boskiej randze. Stopnie Guruttwa pokrywaj¹ siê z opisywanymi oœmioma stopniami duchowej realizacji, oœwiecenia czy wyzwolenia.

TEXT.English: In Turkic mythology, Tengri is a pure, white goose that flies constantly over an endless expanse of water, which represents time. Beneath this water, Ak Ana ("White Mother") calls out to him saying "Create". To overcome his loneliness, Tengri creates Er Kishi, who is not as pure or as white as Tengri and together they set up the world. Er Kishi becomes a demonic character and strives to mislead people and draw them into its darkness. Tengri assumes the name Tengri Ălgen and withdraws into Heaven from which he tries to provide people with guidance through sacred animals that he sends among them. The Ak Tengris occupy the fifth level of Heaven. Shaman priests who want to reach Tengri Ălgen never get further than this level, where they convey their wishes to the divine guides. Returns to earth or to the human level take place in a goose-shaped vessel.

TEXT.English: Houngan is the term for a male priest in Haitian Vodou (a female priest is known as a mambo). The term is derived from the Fon word "hĂčn gan". There are two ranks of houngan: houngan asogwe (high priest) and houngan sur pwen (junior priest). A houngan asogwe is the highest member of clergy in voodoo and the only one with authority to ordain other priests. It is the houngan's role to preserve the rituals and songs and maintain the relationship between the spirits and the community as a whole (though some of this is the responsibility of the whole community as well). They are entrusted with leading the service of all of the spirits of their lineage. Sometimes they may also be bokor (sorcerers).
TEXT.Polish: Houngan to wielki kap³an w religii voodoo na Haiti (jego ¿eñskim odpowiednikiem jest mambo). Hounganie s¹ kap³anami najwy¿szej rangi, prowadz¹ rytua³y wywo³ywania duchów (loa) lub duchów w³asnych zmar³ych przodków. Ich obowi¹zkiem jest podtrzymywanie sta³ej wiêzi miêdzy zmar³ymi i ¿yj¹cymi wyznawcami voodoo. Czuwaj¹ nad zachowaniem tradycji i czystoœci kultu.

TEXT.English: Whaling is the hunting of whales mainly for meat and oil. Its earliest forms date to at least 3000 BC. Various coastal communities have long histories of sustenance whaling and harvesting beached whales. Industrial whaling emerged with organized fleets in the 17th century; competitive national whaling industries in the 18th and 19th centuries; and the introduction of factory ships along with the concept of whale harvesting in the first half of the 20th century.
TEXT.French: La chasse Ă la baleine est la chasse aux baleines principalement pour la viande et l'huile. Ses premiĂšres formes datent d'au moins 3000 ans avant JC. Diverses communautĂ©s cĂŽtiĂšres ont une longue histoire de chasse Ă la baleine de subsistance et de chasse aux baleines Ă©chouĂ©es. La chasse industrielle a Ă©mergĂ© avec des flottes organisĂ©es au 17Ăšme siĂšcle; des industries baleiniĂšres nationales compĂ©titives aux XVIIIe et XIXe siĂšcles; et l'introduction de navires-usines ainsi que le concept de la rĂ©colte des baleines dans la premiĂšre moitiĂ© du 20e siĂšcle.
